compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
Cupid enrichment op: LLM ticket-digest extractor + digest schema.

## Why
Semantic overlap detection needs a compact, normalized per-ticket representation that
catches paraphrase ("login broken" vs "users can't authenticate") WITHOUT embeddings,
keeping rebar on its Anthropic-only, no-heavy-ML grain. The Cupid pattern
(arXiv:2308.10022) uses an LLM as a one-time feature extractor. DERISKED: the recall
experiment (see epic experiment comment) showed digest-BM25F retrieves 8/8 heavy
paraphrases at rank 1 (recall@20 = 1.0); the digest is the load-bearing artifact for the
whole feature.

## What
A new LLM operation `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` returning a validated dedup-digest
wrapper, plus its output schema and prompt. It is a **structured single-turn** op:
assemble the ticket's canonical text -> load the prompt ->
`RunRequest(mode="structured", output_schema="ticket_digest", execution_mode="single_turn")`
-> `get_runner(cfg, override=runner).run(req)` (the runner validates the structured
response against the `ticket_digest` schema via `finalize_outcome`/`validate_structured`)
-> the op post-processes the proposition count and returns the wrapper.

Modeled on the structured single-turn pattern in
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py::pass1_chunk` (which builds
`RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")`) — **NOT** on
`src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (that is a *gate* op that resolves a read-root and delegates
verdict production to the workflow engine; it contains no `RunRequest`), and **NOT** on
`operations.py::review_ticket` (which uses `mode="findings"`/`execution_mode="agentic"`,
the opposite of what we want). Because `enrich` is single-turn with no file/repo tools it
does **NOT** need `gate_source.resolve_gate_handle` / `gate_read_root` or snapshot
materialization; it builds cfg via `LLMConfig.from_env(repo_root=...)` (or an explicit
`config=`), assembles the ticket text, and runs.

The digest schema (`ticket_digest`):
`{problem_keywords: string[], component_or_area: string, key_entities: string[], propositions: string[]}`;
`propositions` = 2-6 atomic problem/repro statements; logs/stack-traces/code are discarded
by the prompt. The payload is deterministic (no timestamps/uuids) so the stored record is
stable modulo LLM wording.

### Op signature (with a body-injection escape hatch for offline tests)
`enrich(ticket_id=None, *, text=None, repo_root=None, config=None, runner=None)`. Exactly
one of `ticket_id` / `text` must be given (else `ValueError`):
- `ticket_id` given -> the op reads the ticket via the established `rebar.llm` facade
  `from rebar import _reads; state = _reads.show_ticket(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` (the
  same layering-correct pattern used by `completion.py:294`, `operations.py:82`,
  `plan_review/orchestrator.py:168`), then assembles the source text with a TRIVIAL INLINE
  join of `state["title"]` + `state["description"]` + each `state["comments"][*]["body"]`
  ("\n"-joined, skipping empties) directly in `enrich.py`. It does NOT import
  `rebar._engine_support.gates._ticket_text` — no `rebar.llm.*` module imports
  `_engine_support.gates`, and doing so would be the first layering violation in the package;
  the join is a couple of lines and needs no private helper. (S1/`2d0f`, which computes a
  content-hash, owns its OWN local normalizer for hashing; enrich's inline join only feeds the
  LLM prompt and need not be byte-identical to it.)
- `text` given -> the op uses that string directly as the source, bypassing the store read
  entirely. This is the injection seam the offline `test_enrich_quality` and other unit
  tests use to feed a fixture `body` without a live ticket store. (It is a real API on the
  op, not a test-only monkeypatch.)

### System prompt + instructions assembly (query context)
- **system_prompt** is resolved from the packaged prompt FILE `reviewers/ticket_digest.md`
  (file stem `ticket_digest` -> prompt id `ticket-digest`, underscores normalized to dashes
  exactly as `ticket_quality.md` -> `ticket-quality`) via the da27 prompt machinery —
  `prompts.get_prompt("ticket-digest", repo_root=cfg.repo_path)` then
  `prompts.resolve_prompt(prompt, {...}, repo_root=cfg.repo_path)` — mirroring
  `plan_review/passes.py::_resolve_system`. No inline prompt string constants; a project
  `.rebar/prompts/ticket-digest.md` override wins.
- **instructions** are the source text resolved above (from `ticket_id` or the injected
  `text`), rendered as plain text. No graph, no file contents. The PROMPT (not the op)
  instructs the model to discard any logs/stack-traces/code that appear in the body — so log
  exclusion is a prompt-encoded guarantee, verified statically by `test_prompt_excludes_logs`.

## Return shape & processing order (grounded in `findings.finalize_outcome`)
`get_runner(cfg, override=runner).run(req)` returns, for `mode="structured"`, the dict
`{**payload, "runner": <str>, "model": <str|None>, "trace_id": <str|None>}` where `payload`
is the model's structured output already validated against the `ticket_digest` schema
(`findings.finalize_outcome` -> `validate_structured`, `findings.py:321`). So the run result
carries the four digest fields PLUS three provenance keys — it is NOT itself a bare digest.
The op therefore:
1. Selects the digest by field name:
   `digest = {k: run_result[k] for k in ("problem_keywords", "component_or_area", "key_entities", "propositions")}`
   (dropping the `runner`/`model`/`trace_id` provenance keys).
2. Post-processes the `propositions` list ONLY: if `len > overlap_propositions_max`, truncate
   to max; if `len < overlap_propositions_min`, keep them all and set
   `low_proposition_count=True`; it never raises on count.
3. Returns the wrapper
   `{"digest": <ticket_digest dict, exactly the four fields>, "low_proposition_count": bool}`.
   `low_proposition_count` lives on the op's RETURN object only — never a fifth field of the
   persisted `ticket_digest`. The `digest` value validates against `ticket_digest`.

## Error contract (resolves AC "clean error, no partial write")
- A shape-invalid structured payload (valid JSON, wrong shape — e.g. `propositions` missing
  or non-string) raises `FindingsError` from `validate_structured` (`findings.py`) INSIDE
  the runner's `run()`, before the op sees any result; the op never catch-and-partial-writes,
  so no record is persisted. This is the path `test_bad_shape` exercises with a FakeRunner
  seeded with a schema-invalid `structured=` dict.
- An absent structured response at runtime (the live model emits no parseable structured
  object) is raised as `rebar.llm.errors.StructuredOutputError` by `finalize_outcome`
  (`findings.py:305`, `structured is None` branch) — a framework guarantee on the live path
  (FakeRunner cannot reproduce it: `FakeRunner(structured=None)` falls through to the
  findings branch, `runner.py:120-137`), so it is asserted at the `finalize_outcome` unit
  level, not fabricated through FakeRunner.
- Absent LLM (no `[agents]` extra / no API key) raises `LLMUnavailableError`/`LLMConfigError`
  (subclasses of `LLMError`) cleanly via the runner preflight/config path; `test_absent_llm`.
- The op defines NO new exception type — it reuses the framework's (`FindingsError`,
  `StructuredOutputError`, `LLMUnavailableError`).

## Config-bound threading (resolves the zero-arg-builder gap)
The proposition bound is NOT enforced through the `register_contract` schema builder (the
zero-arg `Callable[[], type]` cannot see config). Instead: (a) the prompt instructs the
model to emit MIN..MAX propositions, and (b) the op post-validates the parsed count
(truncate to max if more; keep + flag `low_proposition_count` if fewer; never raise). The
MIN/MAX bounds are LLM-feature tunables on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`, env
`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN` / `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MAX`, read in
`LLMConfig.from_env` via the `_llm_int` helper) — `_config_schema.py` reserves the `llm.*`
layer, so these must NOT go on `VerifyConfig`.

## Scope (exact edit targets)
- `src/rebar/llm/config.py`: add module constants `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN = 2` /
  `DEFAULT_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MAX = 6`, dataclass fields
  `LLMConfig.overlap_propositions_min` / `overlap_propositions_max`, and two `_llm_int(...)`
  lines in `from_env` (env `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN/MAX`, file keys
  `overlap_propositions_min/max`).
- New op module `src/rebar/llm/enrich.py` defining
  `enrich(ticket_id=None, *, text=None, repo_root=None, config=None, runner=None)` (exactly
  one of `ticket_id`/`text`; `text` bypasses the store read — see Op signature above).
- Export: add `from rebar.llm.enrich import enrich` to `src/rebar/llm/__init__.py` and add
  `"enrich"` to the `# operations` group of its `__all__` (NOT `src/rebar/__init__.py`; the
  core package must not import `rebar.llm`).
- Prompt `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ticket_digest.md` (stem `ticket_digest` -> id
  `ticket-digest`), front-matter `schema_version: 1`, `execution_mode: single_turn`,
  `outputs: ticket_digest`, `category: enrich` (NOT `review`, so it is not indexed/selected
  as a reviewer — `reviewers/index.json` lists only `category=="review"`), and a body that
  explicitly instructs the model to discard logs/stack-traces/code and emit MIN..MAX atomic
  propositions. The valid `execution_mode` enum is `single_turn|agentic`
  (`prompting/prompts.py:74`); `1-TURN` is invalid. If a new packaged prompt must be
  enumerated in a `_packaged_prompt_files()`/catalog manifest, add `ticket_digest.md` there.
- Output schema `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_digest.schema.json` (all four fields required;
  `problem_keywords`/`key_entities`/`propositions` = arrays of non-empty strings;
  `component_or_area` a non-empty string; `propositions` `minItems: 1`). WIRE IT: in
  `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` add `TICKET_DIGEST = "ticket_digest"`, register
  `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS["enrich"] = TICKET_DIGEST`, and add `TICKET_DIGEST` to `__all__` — this
  satisfies `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py::test_every_schema_file_is_wired`
  (every schema file must appear in `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS.values() | exempt`). The synthetic
  `"enrich"` key has no CLI `--output` arm (like `"verify_completion"`), so the CLI-coverage
  invariants do not drive it.
- Contract registration + bootstrap: define the builder `ticket_digest_response_model()`
  **inside `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py`** (co-located with `completion_verdict_response_model`,
  pydantic imported inside the body), and register it at the BOTTOM of `contracts.py` with
  `register_contract("ticket_digest", ticket_digest_response_model)` next to the two built-ins
  (`review_result`, `completion_verdict`). Registration is guaranteed-before-use by
  **co-location**: `response_model_for` is defined in `contracts.py`, so any code path that
  calls `response_model_for("ticket_digest")` has necessarily imported `contracts.py`, which
  executes the module body — including the bottom `register_contract(...)` line — first. (This
  is the correct mechanism; `rebar.llm.__init__` does NOT import `contracts` at module top —
  the runner imports it lazily — so the guarantee is co-location, not startup import.) Defining
  the builder in `contracts.py` (not `enrich.py`) also avoids a `contracts -> enrich` import
  cycle. `enrich.py` merely references the schema by the string name `"ticket_digest"`.

## Dependencies
None (leaf). S1/S2/S7 consume the op + the `ticket_digest` schema as sibling stories.

## Testing
- Unit `tests/unit/test_enrich.py` (all use `FakeRunner(structured=...)` — NO live LLM):
  - `test_schema_valid` — `enrich()` with a FakeRunner seeded with a valid digest returns
    `{digest, low_proposition_count}` and `digest` validates against `ticket_digest`.
  - `test_no_nondeterministic_fields` — the returned `digest` has exactly the four schema
    fields (no timestamps/uuids/provenance keys leaked from the run result).
  - `test_propositions_bounded` — FakeRunner returning >max propositions => truncated to max;
    <min => kept with `low_proposition_count=True`, no raise.
  - `test_bad_shape` — FakeRunner seeded with a schema-invalid `structured=` dict (e.g.
    `propositions` omitted) => `FindingsError` raised inside the run, and nothing is returned/
    written (no partial write).
  - `test_absent_llm` — with the real (pydantic_ai) runner and no `[agents]`/key, preflight/run
    raises `LLMUnavailableError`/`LLMConfigError` cleanly.
  - `test_enrich_exported` — `from rebar.llm import enrich` succeeds AND
    `rebar.llm.contracts.response_model_for("ticket_digest")` returns the registered
    ticket_digest model (not the default findings model) — covers the export + contract-
    registration Scope items.
  - `test_prompt_excludes_logs` — STATIC assertion that the packaged
    `reviewers/ticket_digest.md` prompt text contains the explicit "discard
    logs/stack-traces/code" directive (the honest place the exclusion guarantee lives — a
    FakeRunner cannot prove model behaviour).
  - `test_prompt_frontmatter` — loads the prompt via `prompts.get_prompt("ticket-digest")`
    and asserts `execution_mode == "single_turn"`, `outputs == "ticket_digest"`, and that it
    is NOT a reviewer (`category != "review"`, absent from `reviewers/index.json`) — so the
    enrich prompt is never selected as a plan/ticket reviewer.
  - `test_finalize_absent_structured` — a `finalize_outcome`-level unit asserting that a
    `structured_response=None` outcome raises `StructuredOutputError` (the live-path guarantee
    FakeRunner cannot reproduce).
- Quality fixture test `test_enrich_quality` — **offline + deterministic, exercises the real
  `enrich()` op path** (assembly -> run -> select -> post-process -> wrap). Fixtures live at
  `tests/fixtures/enrich_quality/*.json`, >=6 files COMMITTED as deliverables of this story,
  each shaped
  `{"ticket_id": str, "body": str (one is a ~4000-token body), "digest": {<pre-generated ticket_digest, four fields, captured once from a real enrich run>}, "gold": [<expected entity/keyword strings>]}`.
  The test FIRST asserts cardinality — `assert len(fixtures) >= 6` (so an empty/missing
  fixture dir fails loudly rather than passing vacuously). Then for each fixture it builds a
  `FakeRunner(structured=fixture["digest"])` and calls `enrich(text=fixture["body"], runner=<fake>)`
  (the `text=` injection seam — no store read), and asserts deterministic string-set
  membership on the RESULT: >=1 `gold` string appears (case-insensitive) in
  `result["digest"]["key_entities"]` union `result["digest"]["problem_keywords"]`. This both
  exercises `enrich()` and stays deterministic (the canned digests are real captured model
  outputs; regenerate only when the prompt changes).
- Gated live regenerator `test_enrich_quality_live` — `@pytest.mark.skipif` unless
  `REBAR_RUN_LLM_EVAL=1`; calls the real model over the fixture bodies and re-asserts gold
  membership (the path that regenerates the canned `digest` blocks). NEVER runs in CI.
- Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py -q` (self-contained;
  fixtures committed, live test skipped, so it never needs a generation step first).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar.llm.enrich(ticket_id)` returns `{digest, low_proposition_count}`, and the
  `digest` field (exactly the four schema fields) validates against `ticket_digest`;
  `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_schema_valid` and `::test_no_nondeterministic_fields`.
- [ ] `from rebar.llm import enrich` succeeds and `contracts.response_model_for("ticket_digest")`
  returns the registered model (not the default findings model);
  `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_enrich_exported`.
- [ ] The packaged `reviewers/ticket_digest.md` prompt encodes the logs/stack-traces/code exclusion
  directive; `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_prompt_excludes_logs`.
- [ ] The `propositions` count is enforced in the op (>max truncate to max; <min keep + flag,
  no raise); `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_propositions_bounded`.
- [ ] A shape-invalid structured payload raises `FindingsError` with no partial write
  (`::test_bad_shape`); an absent structured response raises `StructuredOutputError`
  (`::test_finalize_absent_structured`); absent-LLM raises `LLMUnavailableError`
  (`::test_absent_llm`).
- [ ] The quality fixture test asserts `len(fixtures) >= 6` (fails loudly if the committed
  fixture dir is empty/missing) and, for each, calls `enrich(text=fixture["body"], runner=<fake>)`
  with a FakeRunner seeded with that fixture's captured digest, asserting >=1 gold
  entity/keyword appears in `key_entities` union `problem_keywords` (deterministic; exercises
  the op path via the `text=` injection seam); `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_enrich_quality`.
- [ ] The packaged prompt loads as `ticket-digest` with `execution_mode: single_turn`,
  `outputs: ticket_digest`, and is NOT a reviewer (`category != review`);
  `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich.py::test_prompt_frontmatter`.
compiled_state.title: Cupid enrichment op: LLM ticket-digest extractor + digest schema
